data_coverage_summary: Samsara TMS Integrations is the broadest transportation integration pattern in the catalog, covering two-way sync for addresses, drivers, assets, routes, messages, and forms, plus one-way GPS, telematics, and compliance data for execution visibility.
buyer_guidance: Use TMS Integrations when Last Mile wants a full Samsara transportation partner profile rather than a narrow product-specific feed. This is the best entry for two-way orchestration because the published table explicitly distinguishes which entities are two-way versus read-only.
overview: The TMS integration guide publishes a concrete integration-point matrix. It documents two-way sync for addresses, drivers, vehicles, trailers, routes, messages, and forms through GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE style APIs, while also documenting one-way reads for GPS/telematics and compliance hours-of-service data. It further recommends combining polling, webhooks, and Kafka depending on entity type and update frequency, making this the most architecturally complete Samsara product record in the set.

available_data.description: The guide's sync matrix explicitly marks core dispatch entities as two-way.
The matrix distinguishes live execution data that Samsara emits outward rather than accepts as writes.
data_points: route stop arrival and departure events

available_data.description: The guide documents when to pair APIs with webhooks or Kafka for better freshness.
data_points: Kafka for high-frequency GPS and HOS updates

ingest_considerations: This guide is a pattern-level integration map, so production designs should still break scope down by entity family and token scope.
The published matrix already distinguishes one-way versus two-way sync, which should drive the Supported versus Un-Supported classification for downstream use cases.
Samsara recommends mixing polling, webhooks, and Kafka rather than forcing one transport model across all entities.
Because the breadth is high, this surface should be implemented incrementally, starting with the minimum entity set required for the customer workflow.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: Backed by the TMS integration guide's Integration Points matrix listing two-way sync methods for addresses, drivers, assets, routes, messages, and forms, plus one-way GPS/telematics and HOS methods and associated webhook/Kafka patterns.
